# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_virtualmachineextension (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the vm extension.
- `resource_group` (String) Name of a resource group where the vm extension exists or will be created.
- `virtual_machine_name` (String) The name of the virtual machine where the extension should be create or updated.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_upgrade_minor_version` (Boolean) Whether the extension handler should be automatically upgraded across minor versions during deployment.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enable_automatic_upgrade` (Boolean) Whether the extension handler should be automatically upgraded.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `force_update_tag` (Boolean) Whether the extension should be updated or re-run even if no changes can be detected from what is currently configured. Helpful when applying changes to *protected_settings*.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `location` (String) Valid Azure location. Defaults to location of the resource group.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `protected_settings` (String) JSON formatted protected settings for the extension. Previously configured settings are not available, so the parameter is not used for idempotency checks. If changes to this parameter need to be applied, use in conjunction with *force_update_tag*.
- `publisher` (String) The name of the extension handler publisher.
- `settings` (String) JSON formatted public settings for the extension.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the vm extension. Use `present` to create or update a vm extension and `absent` to delete a vm extension.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type_handler_version` (String) The type version of the extension handler.
- `virtual_machine_extension_type` (String) The type of the extension handler.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
